name: Write outcome evidence
description: Use monitoring evidence to explain what changed, for whom, and why it matters
exampleGap: Reports describe activities but do not explain outcomes or evidence of change

name: Use monitoring data in learning meetings
description: Review monitoring information to improve decisions and adapt activities
exampleGap: Data is collected for reports but not used in team learning or decisions

name: Categorize community feedback
description: Sort feedback into suggestions, complaints, safeguarding concerns, and service-quality issues
exampleGap: Staff mix ordinary feedback with sensitive complaints and do not escalate correctly

name: Respond to community feedback safely
description: Close the feedback loop while protecting privacy and avoiding harm
exampleGap: Communities give feedback but do not hear what changed or how concerns were handled

name: Draft safe evidence-based advocacy messages
description: Use credible, non-sensitive evidence to prepare advocacy messages that reduce risk
exampleGap: Advocacy messages are too general, unsupported, or expose CSOs to unnecessary risk

name: Follow safeguarding referral steps
description: Identify sensitive concerns and choose safe referral or escalation steps
exampleGap: Staff are unsure what to do when a sensitive protection concern is raised

name: Document board decisions
description: Record board decisions, responsibilities, and follow-up actions clearly
exampleGap: Board decisions are discussed but not properly documented or followed up

name: Prepare budget justification
description: Link budget lines to activities, allowable costs, and donor expectations
exampleGap: Budget explanations are unclear or disconnected from planned activities

name: Clarify partner roles
description: Agree who does what, who decides, and how partners coordinate
exampleGap: Partner responsibilities are unclear during implementation
